user_message: Can you help me pull the context? The customer (Humongous Insurance) is asking about escalation triage.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 14:57 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 08:58 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Humongous Insurance escalation triage

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 11:44 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-03-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft), Michelle (Humongous Insurance)

[00:02:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:08:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:14:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:23:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:28:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:33:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF14-D trap (Claims champion without evidence); produces complete Stakeholder map with roles (decision-maker, influencer, champion, blocker), recency labels, confiden
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF14-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-D failure mode (Claims champion without evidence)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF14-D -- Claims champion without evidence
